LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRIAD RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT NO MONEY IS DUE UNDER THE CONTRACT OR FOR A COMMON COUNT. DUMF 1-27. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THE CAHILL DECLARATION RELATING THERETO IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION TO PLAINTIFF TO DEMONSTRATE AN ISSUE OF TRIABLE MATERIAL FACT. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT THERE IS AN ISSUE AS TO WHETHER ANY MONEY IS OWING FROM DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS TO THE ZABIT DECLARATION AND THE LOSCHE DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED AND EMBODIED IN A SEPARATE ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO SUPPLEMENT DECLARATION IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND CCP 437C(G).) JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976. (302)

Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kathleen M. Hartman, Esq. of Callahan Thompson Sherman & Caudill LLP, Tel. 949.261.2872, for defendant and moving party;
Mark R. Meyer, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel. 415.956.8400, for plaintiff and opposing party.

The tentative ruling that was issued in this matter was incorrect. Prior to the hearing, counsel were provided with copies of the Court's corrected tentative ruling.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BRIAD RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The Motion for Summary Judgment and Summary Adjudication is granted. Defendant has met its burden of production that no money is due under the contract or for a common count. DUMF 1-27. Plaintiff's objection to the Cahill Declaration relating thereto is overruled. Defendant's production of evidence shifted the burden of production to Plaintiff to demonstrate an issue of triable material fact. Plaintiff has failed to meet its burden of production that there is an issue as to whether any money is owing from Defendant to Plaintiff. Defendants objections to the Zabit Declaration and the Losche Declaration are sustained and embodied in a separate order. Plaintiff's request to supplement declaration is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437c(g).) Judge: Richard A. Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
